alignments.candidate_evidence: Eight years in commercial operations, scaling at Google Devices and Services with two promotions; experience in customer-facing transformation programs
alignments.job_requirement: Senior Customer Success Manager role in enterprise software context

gaps.gap: Role is core Customer Success account management, not strategic or transformation-focused operations work. Candidate profile lists CS as usually deprioritized without meaningful strategy or transformation scope.
gaps.mitigation: Only viable if JD reveals strategic programs, business transformation, or GTM-operations components within the CS function—but the fetched JD is largely unreadable (HTML rendering artifacts and navigation structure, not actual job content).

gaps.gap: No visible job description content to assess actual scope, responsibilities, or strategic elements. JD appears to be a broken/cached page fetch with no substantive role details.
gaps.mitigation: Request clean, full JD text to evaluate whether this is a pure CS retention-focused account manager role or a strategic/transformation-heavy CS leader position.

gaps.gap: Candidate's core experience is strategy/operations and product transformation, not CS playbook, retention metrics, expansion pipeline, or account health management.
gaps.mitigation: If role requires deep CS expertise, this is a significant domain mismatch.

output.summary: Customer Success account management role in Customer Success department—outside candidate's target and approved families. Fetched JD is unreadable (HTML artifacts), preventing scope assessment. Even if Senior scope qualifies seniority-wise, core CS domain without strategic/transformation framing is explicitly deprioritized in candidate profile.
uncertainties: JD is largely unreadable; unable to extract actual role responsibilities, scope, or whether strategic/transformation components exist.
Unclear whether this is pure account management or whether it includes business transformation, GTM operations, or program leadership scope.
Cannot confirm whether ServiceNow/Moveworks structure places this role as strategic operations or quota/retention-focused CS.
